prompt:
Finance wants the monthly per-service cost rollup as a plain text report they can diff month to month, and I want it as one zsh 5.9 script, `costrep.zsh`, repo root, run as `zsh costrep.zsh <data-file>`. No awk, no bc, no python — zsh does floating point natively and that's the point of this exercise: type the accumulators as floats (typeset -F) or keep the operands float-typed so $(( )) stays in float land; if an integer division sneaks in somewhere the mean comes out truncated and finance notices.

Input is one billing item per line: `<service> <amount>`, columns separated by spaces or tabs. The same service can appear on several lines — amounts accumulate under one entry. Amounts are plain decimals: optional leading minus, digits, optionally a dot with digits after it. `007` is fine (it's seven), `5.`, `.5`, `1e3`, and words are not — those lines get `costrep.zsh: line <N>: bad row, skipped` on stderr (N counts every physical line from 1) and processing continues; warnings never change the exit code. Lines starting with # and blank lines are padding, skipped silently.

The report, tab-separated, byte-exact:

rows	<accepted line count>
services	<distinct service count>
total	<sum, %.2f>
mean	<total divided by services, %.2f>
min	<service>	<its accumulated amount, %.2f>
max	<service>	<amount, %.2f>
SHARES
<service>	<accumulated amount as percent of total, %.2f>   (one per service, first-seen order)

Min and max compare the accumulated per-service amounts; on a tie the service seen earliest in the file wins both. If the total is exactly zero, print 0.00 for every share instead of dividing — zsh float division by zero hands you Inf, not an error, and Inf in a finance report is a career moment.

Two output disciplines to get right. First, rounding: use printf '%.2f' and let it do the work — on this box it rounds an exact half to the even digit (0.125 prints as 0.12, 0.375 as 0.38), the tests bake those exact bytes in, so don't bolt on your own rounding arithmetic. Second, locale: the script itself must pin the numeric locale (LC_ALL=C works) so the report prints decimal points even when the caller's environment says comma — the harness runs one case under de_DE to make sure.

No usable rows at all (empty file or everything skipped) is `costrep.zsh: no data rows` on stderr, exit 65. No argument: `usage: costrep.zsh <data-file>` on stderr, exit 64. Unreadable file: `costrep.zsh: cannot read: <arg>` on stderr, exit 66.

`zsh test_costrep.zsh` from the workspace root is the acceptance gate; it must pass unmodified.
